prompts.title: SMART IEP Goals for Grade 4 Student with ASD
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with 12 years of experience working in CBSE inclusive schools in India and RCI-registered. I need you to draft a complete set of SMART IEP goals for Arjun, a 9-year-old Grade 4 student with Autism Spectrum Disorder (Level 2), who has limited expressive language (about 30 functional words), struggles with transitions, and shows strength in visual pattern recognition. His parents speak Hindi at home and want bilingual support. Please provide: (1) Two annual academic goals (literacy and numeracy) with 4 quarterly benchmarks each, (2) Two communication goals aligned with PECS Phase 3-4, (3) One social-emotional goal, (4) One self-help/adaptive goal aligned with RPwD Act 2016 definitions, (5) For each goal: baseline data, measurable criteria (e.g., "80% accuracy across 4 of 5 trials"), evaluation method, person responsible, and materials. Format as a structured table with columns: Domain, Annual Goal, Baseline, Benchmarks Q1-Q4, Evaluation Tool, Responsible Staff. Consider NIEPID curriculum frameworks, availability of low-cost TLMs in Indian schools, and ensure goals respect family language preferences. Include suggested review dates aligned with Indian academic calendar (April-March).

prompts.title: Sensory Diet for ADHD Student in Indian Classroom
prompts.prompt: Act as an occupational therapist and special educator with 10 years of experience in Indian inclusive schools. I need you to design a complete daily sensory diet for Priya, a 7-year-old Grade 2 student with ADHD (combined type) who seeks proprioceptive input, gets overstimulated during assembly, and has difficulty remaining seated during 40-minute periods. The school has limited OT equipment but access to a playground, a small resource room, and basic materials. Please provide: (1) A minute-by-minute sensory schedule from 8:00 AM to 2:30 PM aligned with a typical Indian school timetable including assembly, periods, recess and lunch, (2) 10 low-cost classroom sensory activities using Indian materials (rice bins, atta dough, dal trays, theraband alternatives), (3) 5 heavy-work tasks Priya can perform as classroom helper roles, (4) A traffic-light self-regulation chart in English and Hindi, (5) Teacher cues and discreet signals to avoid stigmatizing the child, (6) Guidance for parents for weekend carryover. Format as a visual schedule table plus activity cards. Consider Indian classroom sizes (40+ students), limited space, cultural sensitivity around touch, and RCI-endorsed sensory integration principles.

prompts.title: Tiered Differentiated Math Lesson Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with UDL expertise working in an ICSE inclusive school in Pune. I need you to create a tiered, differentiated 45-minute math lesson on fractions (halves, quarters, thirds) for a Grade 5 classroom of 38 students that includes 4 students with learning disabilities, 2 with mild intellectual disability, 1 with ADHD, and 1 with low vision. Please provide: (1) A single common learning objective plus tiered sub-objectives for Tier 1, Tier 2, Tier 3 learners, (2) Three parallel instructional pathways (concrete using Indian materials like roti, mithai boxes, measuring cups; representational using diagrams; abstract using symbols), (3) UDL-aligned multiple means of engagement, representation, and expression, (4) Low-vision accommodations (tactile fraction kits, enlarged print 24pt), (5) Differentiated worksheets at 3 levels, (6) Exit ticket with 3 difficulty versions, (7) Co-teaching roles for general and special educator. Format as a structured lesson plan template. Consider NCERT Grade 5 math curriculum alignment, time constraints of Indian classrooms, and budget constraints (under Rs 500 for materials).

prompts.title: Functional Behavior Assessment Report
prompts.prompt: Act as a Board Certified Behavior Analyst equivalent special educator trained under RCI guidelines with 8 years experience in Indian special schools. I need you to conduct a complete Functional Behavior Assessment (FBA) write-up for Rahul, an 11-year-old with intellectual disability (moderate) who exhibits aggressive behaviors (hitting peers, throwing objects) approximately 6 times per day, primarily during unstructured transitions and math period. Please provide: (1) Operational definitions of target behaviors, (2) ABC data collection template for 2 weeks (minimum 20 incidents), (3) Scatter plot template to identify time patterns, (4) Hypothesis statement of function (escape, attention, tangible, sensory), (5) Setting events analysis, (6) Summary statement in standard FBA format, (7) Three recommended function-based interventions, (8) Data-based decision criteria for fading. Format as a professional FBA report Indian schools can file in student records. Consider Indian disability categories under RPwD Act 2016, cultural sensitivity around behavior management (no corporal punishment per RTE Act), and parent communication in simple English or Hindi.

prompts.title: Behavior Intervention Plan with Reinforcement Schedule
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with 10 years experience in NIEPID-affiliated schools. I need you to draft a complete Behavior Intervention Plan (BIP) for Kavya, a 6-year-old with Down syndrome who engages in elopement (leaving seat/classroom) an average of 8 times per 90-minute session, hypothesized function: escape from difficult tasks. Please provide: (1) Operational behavior definition, (2) Replacement behavior (functional communication request for break using PECS card or sign), (3) Antecedent strategies (task modification, choice boards, visual schedules, priming), (4) Teaching procedures for the replacement behavior using errorless learning, (5) Reinforcement schedule starting with FR1 and systematic thinning to VR4 over 6 weeks, (6) Reactive strategies that avoid reinforcing escape, (7) Crisis plan aligned with Indian school safety norms, (8) Data collection sheet and mastery criteria, (9) Staff training checklist. Format as an implementable BIP document. Consider typical Indian classroom resources, parental involvement culture, and ethical guidelines from RCI.

prompts.title: Progress Monitoring Data System for IEP Goals
prompts.prompt: Act as a special education coordinator with 15 years experience managing IEPs in Indian CBSE schools. I need you to design a comprehensive progress monitoring system for a caseload of 12 students with varying disabilities (3 ASD, 4 LD, 2 ID, 2 ADHD, 1 CP). Please provide: (1) A weekly data collection schedule rotating across goals, (2) Three data collection methods appropriate for different goal types (frequency count, duration, percentage correct, rubric-based), (3) Google Sheets template structure with tabs for each student, (4) Graphing format showing baseline, intervention phase, and trend lines with aim lines, (5) Data-based decision rules (3-point rule, 4-point rule) for when to modify interventions, (6) Monthly progress report template for parents in simple language, (7) Quarterly IEP review meeting agenda, (8) Annual review data summary format. Format as a complete system documentation. Consider Indian school technology constraints, typical special educator caseload ratios per RPwD guidelines (1:8), and CBSE documentation requirements for inclusive schools.

prompts.title: IEP Goals for Specific Learning Disability (Dyslexia)
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator certified by RCI with expertise in dyslexia remediation using Orton-Gillingham principles. I need you to write annual IEP goals for Ishaan, a 10-year-old Grade 5 student with Specific Learning Disability (dyslexia), reading at Grade 2 level with Hindi as L1 and English as school medium. His baseline: reads 30 WCPM in English, 50% accuracy on CVC spelling, difficulty with blends and digraphs. Please provide: (1) Three reading goals (decoding, fluency, comprehension) with SMART criteria and benchmarks, (2) Two writing goals (spelling, written expression), (3) One Hindi literacy goal leveraging L1 strengths, (4) Assistive technology recommendations available in India (Read&Write, Indian text-to-speech apps), (5) Accommodations list for CBSE board exams under Differently Abled category, (6) Suggested structured literacy intervention minutes per week, (7) Progress monitoring tools (DIBELS-like, curriculum-based measurement). Format as an IEP goals page. Consider CBSE Class 10 exam accommodation procedures, RPwD Act SLD recognition, and bilingual learner needs.

prompts.title: Differentiated Science Lesson on the Water Cycle
prompts.prompt: Act as an inclusive education specialist with UDL certification teaching in a Kerala government school. I need you to create a differentiated 40-minute science lesson on the water cycle for a Grade 4 class of 42 students including 3 with intellectual disability (mild), 2 with ASD, 1 hearing impaired student (uses ISL), and 5 struggling readers. Please provide: (1) Common objective plus tiered objectives, (2) Multi-sensory instructional sequence (video with captions, hands-on demo using kettle and ice, tactile water cycle wheel), (3) Vocabulary pre-teaching in English and Malayalam with visuals, (4) ISL signs for key terms (evaporation, condensation, precipitation), (5) Three leveled graphic organizers, (6) Partner grouping strategy for peer support, (7) Three assessment options (verbal, drawing, written), (8) Home-learning extension using kitchen observation. Format as a structured UDL lesson plan. Consider NCERT environmental studies curriculum, regional language integration, low-resource rural school context, and RPwD inclusive education mandate.

prompts.title: Sensory Room Setup on Indian School Budget
prompts.prompt: Act as a special education consultant with experience setting up sensory rooms in budget-constrained Indian schools. I need you to design a complete sensory room for a special school in Jaipur with a 12x12 ft unused room and a budget of Rs 40,000. The school serves 25 students aged 5-15 with ASD, ID, CP, and sensory processing differences. Please provide: (1) Zoned floor plan (calming zone, alerting zone, vestibular zone, tactile zone, visual zone), (2) Complete equipment list with Indian suppliers and INR prices (crash mats, theraputty alternatives, fiber optic lights, weighted lap pads, swings safe for CP), (3) DIY low-cost alternatives using locally available materials, (4) Safety protocols and supervision ratios, (5) Scheduling template for 25 students across a school day, (6) Staff training topics, (7) Cleaning and maintenance plan, (8) Parent orientation guide. Format as a project proposal with budget spreadsheet. Consider Indian safety standards, availability of assistive tech through ALIMCO, hot climate considerations, and dust-resistant materials.

prompts.title: Token Economy System for ADHD Classroom
prompts.prompt: Act as a behavior specialist with RCI registration and 9 years experience in Indian inclusive schools. I need you to design a classroom-wide token economy for a Grade 3 class of 35 students that includes 4 students with ADHD and 2 with ODD features. Please provide: (1) Three target classroom behaviors operationally defined in student-friendly language, (2) Token type appropriate for Indian context (stickers, stamps, beads in a jar), (3) Individual and group contingencies, (4) Reinforcement menu with free/low-cost backup reinforcers culturally relevant to Indian kids (extra cricket time, choosing assembly song, class monitor role, Bollywood dance break), (5) Delivery schedule starting FR2 and thinning, (6) Response cost rules with ethical safeguards (no corporal punishment, no public shaming), (7) Data tracking sheet, (8) Fading plan to natural reinforcement over 8 weeks, (9) Parent communication format. Format as an implementation guide. Consider RTE Act protections, avoiding comparison culture pitfalls in Indian classrooms, and sustainability.

prompts.title: Transition IEP for Adolescent with Autism
prompts.prompt: Act as a transition specialist with NIEPID training and 12 years experience supporting adolescents with disabilities in India. I need you to draft a transition-focused IEP for Neha, a 16-year-old with high-functioning autism in Class 11 CBSE (Commerce stream), who has strong rote memory, anxiety in social situations, and interest in graphic design. Her family wants her to pursue higher education or vocational training. Please provide: (1) Person-centered transition assessment summary, (2) Post-secondary vision statement, (3) Three measurable post-school goals (education, employment, independent living), (4) Annual IEP goals that link to post-school goals, (5) Transition services list (career exploration, self-advocacy training, social skills group, work-based learning), (6) Indian post-school options research (NIOS, skill development courses, NSDC programs, inclusive colleges like St. Xavier's Mumbai), (7) Disability certificate and UDID card action steps, (8) Family involvement plan. Format as transition IEP pages. Consider RPwD Act higher education provisions, 5% reservation, and realistic vocational pathways in India.

prompts.title: Visual Schedule System for Non-Verbal Student
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with AAC expertise working in a Chennai special school. I need you to design a comprehensive visual schedule system for Karthik, a 7-year-old non-verbal boy with severe autism who struggles with transitions and unexpected changes. Please provide: (1) First-Then board template with Tamil and English labels, (2) Full-day visual schedule with 12 activity cards using real photos and SymbolStix-style icons, (3) Now-Next-Then board for morning routine, (4) Transition warning system (5-min, 2-min, 1-min countdown cards), (5) Choice boards for snack, play, and calm-down activities, (6) Mini-schedules for bathroom routine and handwashing, (7) Change card and surprise card with teaching script, (8) Implementation fidelity checklist for teachers, (9) Home version for parents with Tamil instructions, (10) Printable templates description (A4 size, laminated, Velcro). Format as an implementation toolkit. Consider Indian cultural visuals (Indian food, clothing, settings), availability of printing resources, and inclusion of extended family members common in Indian homes.

prompts.title: Progress Report for Parent-Teacher Meeting
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with 7 years experience writing progress reports for Indian parents. I need you to draft a quarterly progress report for Ananya, a 9-year-old with mild intellectual disability in an inclusive Grade 3 classroom, covering the July-September quarter. Her IEP has 6 goals across literacy, numeracy, social skills, and self-help. Please provide: (1) Parent-friendly narrative summary (300 words, simple English, avoid jargon), (2) Goal-by-goal data table with baseline, target, current level, and percentage progress, (3) Three visual graphs described for each academic domain, (4) Strengths celebration section with specific examples, (5) Areas needing more support with concrete evidence, (6) Home-support suggestions (3 activities parents can do in 15 minutes daily), (7) Next quarter focus areas, (8) PTM discussion agenda, (9) Hindi translation notes for key terms. Format as a printable 2-page report. Consider Indian parent culture around academic comparison, sensitivity in framing challenges, and building trust with families new to special education.

prompts.title: Social Stories Bank for Common School Scenarios
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with Carol Gray Social Stories certification and experience in Indian schools. I need you to write a bank of 10 social stories for Indian students with autism aged 6-12 covering common school scenarios. Please provide 10 complete social stories for: (1) Morning assembly and national anthem, (2) Going to school in an auto-rickshaw or school bus, (3) Eating lunch from a tiffin with friends, (4) Fire drill procedures, (5) Substitute teacher day, (6) Celebrating Diwali at school, (7) Sports day participation, (8) Using the school bathroom, (9) Asking for help from a teacher, (10) When a friend is absent. Each story should follow Gray's ratio (descriptive: directive 2:1 or higher), use first person, present tense, and include 8-12 sentences with suggested visuals. Format each story as a printable page with title, story text, and visual placeholder descriptions. Consider Indian school culture, names, routines (prayer, pledge), and include 2-3 Hindi/regional translation notes per story.

prompts.title: ABC Data Collection Analysis
prompts.prompt: Act as a behavior analyst with RCI credentials analyzing classroom behavior data. I need you to analyze 2 weeks of ABC data I will describe for Varun, a 10-year-old with ADHD and ODD who exhibits work refusal. The data shows: 18 incidents, 14 occurring during writing tasks, 12 following teacher instructions, 16 followed by removal to principal's office. Please provide: (1) Summary of patterns (antecedent, behavior topography, consequence), (2) Hypothesized function with supporting evidence, (3) Alternative explanations ruled out, (4) Recommended antecedent modifications (task demands, choice, priming), (5) Replacement behavior to teach, (6) Consequence changes that don't reinforce the function, (7) Three specific, testable intervention strategies ranked by feasibility in Indian classroom, (8) Data collection plan to verify function hypothesis, (9) Decision rules for 2-week intervention review. Format as a professional behavior consultation report. Consider Indian school disciplinary norms, avoiding exclusionary practices per RTE Act, and teacher capacity in large classrooms.

prompts.title: Task Analysis for Self-Care Skills
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator trained in applied behavior analysis with experience teaching functional skills at NIEPID. I need you to create detailed task analyses for 5 critical self-care skills for a 12-year-old boy with moderate intellectual disability. Please provide complete task analyses for: (1) Brushing teeth (15-20 steps), (2) Washing hands with soap, (3) Eating with spoon from a thali, (4) Using Indian-style toilet independently, (5) Putting on school uniform including tying shoelaces. For each task analysis include: step-by-step breakdown, prompting hierarchy (verbal, gestural, model, physical), chaining method recommended (forward, backward, total task), error correction procedure, mastery criteria (e.g., 100% independent for 3 consecutive days), data sheet template, and generalization plan across home and school settings. Format as a skills training manual. Consider Indian cultural practices (use of water vs toilet paper, eating with hands vs utensils), materials typically available, and parent training handover.

prompts.title: Accommodations Letter for CBSE Board Exams
prompts.prompt: Act as a special education coordinator with experience securing CBSE Class 10 and 12 board exam accommodations for students with disabilities. I need you to draft a complete application letter and supporting documentation for Meera, a 15-year-old Class 10 student with Specific Learning Disability (dyslexia and dysgraphia), UDID card holder, requesting accommodations. Please provide: (1) Formal application letter to CBSE Regional Office, (2) List of 8 requested accommodations with justifications (scribe, extra time 60 minutes, computer with spell-check disabled, separate room, reader, exemption from third language, bullet point answers accepted, use of calculator), (3) Supporting documentation checklist (UDID card, psycho-educational assessment by RCI professional, school principal certificate, previous accommodations record), (4) Timeline for application submission aligned with CBSE deadlines, (5) Follow-up procedure if denied, (6) Student preparation plan for using accommodations, (7) Scribe eligibility criteria per CBSE circular, (8) Parent instruction sheet. Format as a ready-to-submit application packet. Consider latest CBSE circulars on CWSN, RPwD Act mandates, and appeals process.

prompts.title: Inclusive PE Lesson for Student with Cerebral Palsy
prompts.prompt: Act as an adapted physical education specialist with 8 years experience in Indian inclusive schools. I need you to plan a 40-minute inclusive PE lesson on throwing and catching skills for a Grade 6 class of 40 students including Aditya, who has spastic diplegic cerebral palsy, uses a walker, and has limited lower body mobility but good upper body strength. Please provide: (1) Inclusive learning objectives with participation-based alternatives, (2) Warm-up modifications (seated alternatives), (3) Skill progression from easy to challenging using UDL, (4) Three throwing game variations where Aditya can fully participate (seated throwing station, partner activities, small-sided games), (5) Equipment adaptations (lightweight balls, velcro mitts, larger targets), (6) Peer buddy system guidance, (7) Safety considerations for walker use in playground, (8) Assessment rubric that measures individual progress not comparison, (9) Parent communication about physical activity benefits. Format as an inclusive PE lesson plan. Consider Indian school playground conditions, hot weather adaptations, and availability of adapted equipment through ALIMCO.

prompts.title: Communication Goals Using PECS Protocol
prompts.prompt: Act as a PECS-trained special educator with 10 years experience in Indian ASD programs. I need you to write detailed IEP goals and an implementation plan for teaching PECS Phases 1-4 to Siddharth, a 5-year-old non-verbal child with autism who currently grabs items without requesting. Please provide: (1) One annual communication goal with phase-by-phase objectives, (2) Baseline assessment procedure, (3) Phase 1 goal (single picture exchange) with mastery criteria, (4) Phase 2 goal (distance and persistence), (5) Phase 3A and 3B goals (discrimination), (6) Phase 4 goal (sentence structure "I want"), (7) Reinforcer assessment using Indian context items (Parle-G biscuits, bubbles, balloons, Hindi rhymes), (8) Two-trainer setup for initial training, (9) Data collection per trial, (10) Generalization across people, settings, and items, (11) Parent coaching plan for home implementation. Format as a PECS program guide. Consider Indian family involvement, printing and laminating picture cards on budget, and cultural food/toy examples.

prompts.title: Universal Design Learning Unit Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as a UDL expert special educator working with CBSE curriculum in a Mumbai school. I need you to design a complete 3-week UDL unit plan on "Our Freedom Struggle" for Grade 8 social studies in a class of 45 students with diverse learners including 3 with LD, 1 with ASD, 2 with ADHD, and several English language learners. Please provide: (1) Unit essential questions and enduring understandings, (2) UDL checkpoint analysis for all three principles (engagement, representation, action/expression), (3) Day-by-day lesson overview (15 lessons), (4) Multiple means of representation (texts at 3 reading levels, videos with captions, audio in Hindi and English, infographics, museum field trip), (5) Multiple means of engagement (choice boards, interest-based groupings, real-world connections to current events), (6) Multiple means of expression (essay, podcast, graphic novel, speech, diorama, role-play), (7) Flexible assessment rubric, (8) Required technology and low-tech alternatives, (9) Home-school connection activities. Format as a comprehensive UDL unit plan. Consider NCERT history curriculum, bilingual student support, and Indian patriotic sensitivities.

prompts.title: Calm-Down Corner Design and Protocol
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator specializing in emotional regulation for primary grade students in Indian schools. I need you to design a complete calm-down corner for a Grade 1 inclusive classroom that serves 40 students including 2 with ASD, 1 with ADHD, and several with trauma backgrounds. Please provide: (1) Physical setup in a 4x4 ft corner (visual barrier, soft seating, lighting), (2) Sensory toolkit with 10 low-cost items (stress balls, chikki textures, squeeze bottles, fidget spinners alternatives under Rs 50 each), (3) Visual emotion chart with 5 feelings in English and Hindi, (4) Step-by-step calming strategies card (breathing, counting, body scan, mantra), (5) Timer system (3-5 minutes max), (6) Entry protocol (student-initiated and teacher-prompted), (7) Exit protocol with check-in, (8) Usage data tracking sheet, (9) Teaching script for whole class introduction, (10) Do's and don'ts list. Format as a classroom implementation guide. Consider avoiding stigma, using it as proactive not punitive, and cultural comfort with practices like simple pranayama.

prompts.title: IEP Goals for Student with Cerebral Palsy
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with experience working with motor disabilities in Indian schools. I need you to draft IEP goals for Ravi, an 8-year-old Grade 3 student with spastic quadriplegic cerebral palsy (GMFCS Level IV), non-verbal (uses eye-gaze and yes/no signals), cognitively at Grade 2 level, attends an inclusive CBSE school. Please provide: (1) Three communication goals using low-tech AAC and working toward high-tech device, (2) Two academic goals (literacy and math) with physical access accommodations, (3) One fine motor goal with OT collaboration, (4) One social goal with peer interaction, (5) Assistive technology recommendations (eye-gaze device, switches, Tobii alternatives, locally available options), (6) Positioning and physical access plan for classroom, (7) Related services required (PT, OT, speech), (8) Accommodations list, (9) Emergency evacuation plan. Format as an IEP document. Consider RPwD Act benchmark disability provisions, ALIMCO assistive device options, inclusive classroom physical barriers in Indian schools, and family transport considerations.

prompts.title: Data Collection Sheet for Multiple Goals
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator managing IEP data for a caseload in an Indian special school. I need you to create a single-page daily data collection sheet that allows me to track progress for one student across 8 IEP goals efficiently during a 6-hour school day. The student is a 9-year-old with moderate ID, and goals span literacy, math, communication, social, self-help, and behavior. Please provide: (1) Printable A4 layout design with all goals on one page, (2) Quick-mark coding system (I=independent, V=verbal prompt, G=gesture, M=model, P=physical, R=refusal), (3) Frequency count boxes for behavior goals, (4) Duration tracking for attention goals, (5) Percentage calculation section, (6) Notes column for anecdotal data, (7) Daily summary row, (8) Weekly rollup tab reference, (9) Staff initials and timestamp fields, (10) Tips for reliability and inter-rater agreement. Format as a template description with field-by-field specifications. Consider realistic teacher time in large Indian classrooms, paper-based context, and ease of transfer to digital records.

prompts.title: Social Skills Group Curriculum
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with social skills training expertise in Indian inclusive schools. I need you to design an 8-week social skills group curriculum for 6 students aged 10-12 with ASD and social communication difficulties attending an ICSE school in Bangalore. Please provide: (1) Pre-assessment tool (social skills checklist), (2) Group norms and visual rules, (3) Week-by-week session plans (45 minutes each) covering: greetings, conversation starters, understanding emotions, perspective-taking, friendship skills, handling teasing, group projects, self-advocacy, (4) Each session structure (warm-up, lesson, practice, review, homework), (5) Role-play scenarios using Indian school contexts (canteen, classroom group work, cricket ground), (6) Video modeling suggestions, (7) Reinforcement system, (8) Parent handout per week, (9) Post-assessment and generalization plan, (10) Teacher coordination checklist. Format as a curriculum manual. Consider Indian social norms (namaste greetings, hierarchical respect, joint family dynamics), peer culture, and avoiding stigmatizing labels.

prompts.title: Reinforcement Inventory for Student Assessment
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator conducting reinforcer assessments at an Indian special school. I need you to create a comprehensive reinforcer inventory tool and protocol for identifying effective reinforcers for students with autism and intellectual disabilities aged 5-15. Please provide: (1) Parent/caregiver questionnaire with 30 items covering food, toys, activities, sensory, social preferences culturally relevant to India, (2) Teacher observation form, (3) Student preference assessment protocols (free operant, single stimulus, paired stimulus, multiple stimulus with and without replacement), (4) 40-item reinforcer menu organized by category (edibles like Parle-G, Kurkure, chocolate; tangibles like stickers, small toys; activities like tablet time, swinging; sensory like vibrating pen, light-up toys; social like high-five, hug, praise), (5) Preference hierarchy ranking sheet, (6) Reinforcer rotation schedule to prevent satiation, (7) Ethical considerations (avoiding coercion, respecting dietary/religious restrictions like Jain vegetarianism), (8) Retesting schedule. Format as an assessment toolkit. Consider Indian dietary diversity and cultural sensitivities.

prompts.title: Crisis De-escalation Protocol for School
prompts.prompt: Act as a behavior specialist with crisis prevention training advising an Indian inclusive school. I need you to develop a complete crisis de-escalation protocol for managing a student with intellectual disability who occasionally has aggressive meltdowns (hitting, biting) lasting 10-30 minutes. Please provide: (1) Crisis cycle model explanation (calm, trigger, agitation, acceleration, peak, de-escalation, recovery), (2) Early warning sign checklist, (3) Proactive strategies during calm phase, (4) De-escalation techniques at each phase (space, reduced language, choices, diversion), (5) Safe physical environment modifications, (6) Staff roles during crisis (lead, assist, clear room, call for help), (7) When and how to call parents, (8) Post-crisis recovery support for student and staff, (9) Incident documentation form aligned with Indian school policies, (10) Debriefing protocol, (11) Prohibitions (no corporal punishment, no restraint without training, no seclusion), (12) Staff training outline. Format as a school safety protocol document. Consider RTE Act prohibitions, RPwD safeguards, and cultural norms around physical touch.

prompts.title: Home Program for Parent Training
prompts.prompt: Act as a parent trainer with NIEPID-style family-centered approach and 10 years experience. I need you to create a 6-week home program for the parents of Tanvi, a 4-year-old with autism, recently diagnosed, Hindi-speaking middle-class family in Lucknow. The family is motivated but overwhelmed. Please provide: (1) Week 1: Understanding autism and setting up environment (15 minutes/day tasks), (2) Week 2: Establishing routines and visual supports, (3) Week 3: Building joint attention and engagement, (4) Week 4: Teaching requests using PECS/signs, (5) Week 5: Managing challenging behaviors proactively, (6) Week 6: Daily living skills and generalization, (7) Each week include: learning objective, 3 simple activities with materials list (under Rs 200), parent practice video descriptions, reflection questions, common mistakes to avoid, (8) WhatsApp check-in schedule, (9) Resource list (Action for Autism Delhi, government schemes, UDID application), (10) Self-care reminders for parents. Format as a parent handbook with weekly modules. Consider Hindi language support, joint family dynamics, and cost constraints.

prompts.title: Differentiated Reading Groups for Grade 2
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with structured literacy expertise in a Hyderabad inclusive primary school. I need you to design a 60-minute differentiated reading block for a Grade 2 class of 36 students divided into 4 leveled groups, including 5 students with reading difficulties (2 with diagnosed dyslexia). Please provide: (1) Group composition with reading levels (emergent, early, transitional, fluent), (2) Rotation schedule with 15-minute stations (teacher-led, word work, independent reading, technology), (3) Lesson plan for each group during teacher-led station, (4) Phonics progression for struggling readers using Orton-Gillingham sequence, (5) Decodable text suggestions available in India, (6) Word work activities with magnetic letters, sand trays, whiteboards, (7) Independent reading expectations with accountability, (8) Progress monitoring using running records weekly, (9) Intervention students receive double-dose with 1:1 or 1:2 ratio, (10) Materials list under Rs 1000. Format as a literacy block guide. Consider NCERT Grade 2 language expectations, English as second language context, and practical classroom management in 40+ student rooms.

prompts.title: IEP Meeting Agenda and Facilitation Script
prompts.prompt: Act as a special education coordinator facilitating an IEP meeting in an Indian CBSE school. I need you to prepare a complete IEP meeting package for Rohan's annual review, an 11-year-old with ADHD and anxiety, attended by parents (one English-speaking father, Hindi-speaking mother), class teacher, special educator, school counselor, and principal. Please provide: (1) Meeting agenda with time allocations (60 minutes total), (2) Pre-meeting preparation checklist, (3) Welcoming script that builds rapport, (4) Strength-first data presentation format, (5) Parent input elicitation questions (simple Hindi translations), (6) Goal review structure with visual aids, (7) New goal drafting collaborative process, (8) Accommodations and services discussion, (9) Disagreement resolution approach, (10) Action items and responsible persons table, (11) Next steps and follow-up timeline, (12) Meeting minutes template, (13) Parent summary document in plain language. Format as a facilitator's guide. Consider Indian cultural norms around authority and professional deference, importance of inviting grandparents when significant, and keeping meetings respectful and solution-focused.

prompts.title: Self-Advocacy Curriculum for Teens
prompts.prompt: Act as a transition specialist teaching self-advocacy to Indian teenagers with disabilities. I need you to design a 10-lesson self-advocacy curriculum for a group of 8 high school students (ages 14-17) with various disabilities including LD, ADHD, and ASD at a Delhi CBSE school. Please provide: (1) Lesson 1: Understanding my disability, (2) Lesson 2: My rights under RPwD Act 2016, (3) Lesson 3: Knowing my strengths and needs, (4) Lesson 4: Requesting accommodations, (5) Lesson 5: Disclosing my disability (when, how, to whom), (6) Lesson 6: Navigating UDID and disability certificate, (7) Lesson 7: Participating in my own IEP meeting, (8) Lesson 8: College and career planning with disability, (9) Lesson 9: Mental health and stress management, (10) Lesson 10: Building support network and mentors. For each 45-minute lesson include: objectives, discussion prompts, activity, role-play scenario, reflection journal prompt, home task. Format as a curriculum guide. Consider Indian hierarchy culture challenges in self-advocacy, stigma around disability, and empowerment framing.

prompts.title: Morning Circle Routine for Special Class
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator at a NIEPID-affiliated special school running a self-contained classroom. I need you to design a 30-minute structured morning circle routine for 10 students aged 6-9 with varied disabilities (5 ASD, 3 ID, 2 Down syndrome). Please provide: (1) Complete routine sequence (greeting, attendance, calendar, weather, feelings check, news, song, story, preview of day), (2) Visual supports for each segment (icons, real photos), (3) Multi-sensory engagement at each step (tactile, auditory, visual, movement), (4) Differentiated participation levels for each student based on ability, (5) Peer interaction opportunities, (6) Embedded IEP goals (colors, counting, name recognition, greetings, requesting), (7) Behavioral expectations and visual rules, (8) Song and finger-play suggestions in English and Hindi, (9) Transition cues to next activity, (10) Data collection during circle (participation, responding, attending), (11) Staff roles (lead teacher, para support), (12) Parent newsletter blurb describing what we practice. Format as an implementation guide. Consider Indian cultural songs, prayers/invocations appropriate to a secular or faith-based school, and sensory regulation for diverse learners.

prompts.title: Assessment Report for IEP Development
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with RCI certification writing a comprehensive educational assessment report. I need you to draft a complete assessment report for Sneha, a 7-year-old girl referred for evaluation due to reading and writing difficulties, attention concerns, and social withdrawal at a Kolkata English-medium school. Please provide: (1) Background information section (referral source, family history, medical, developmental milestones), (2) Assessment tools used (informal reading inventory, WIAT-like subtests available in India, observation, parent interview, teacher rating scales), (3) Cognitive functioning summary (inferred from classroom performance), (4) Academic achievement results (reading, writing, math with percentile equivalents), (5) Behavioral and social-emotional observations, (6) Strengths and areas of concern, (7) Diagnostic impression aligned with RPwD categories (suspected SLD, potential ADHD), (8) Recommendations for IEP team (goals, services, accommodations), (9) Referral suggestions (pediatrician, clinical psychologist for formal diagnosis), (10) Parent summary in simple language. Format as a professional assessment report suitable for school records. Consider RCI ethics, avoiding diagnosis without qualified professionals, and culturally appropriate assessment.

prompts.title: Low-Cost TLM Ideas for Indian Special Schools
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with 15 years experience creating teaching-learning materials for resource-constrained Indian special schools. I need you to compile 25 low-cost or no-cost TLM ideas using locally available materials for teaching core skills to students with intellectual disabilities. Please provide 25 TLM ideas organized by skill area: (1) Five for sorting and matching (bottle caps, buttons, dals, pulses), (2) Five for pre-writing and fine motor (chalkboard slates, sand trays, clothespins, dough), (3) Five for number sense and counting (abacus from beads, number strips, bottle cap addition), (4) Five for reading readiness (flashcards from old calendars, matching games from newspapers, letter stamps from cut vegetables), (5) Five for communication and language (picture cards from old magazines, emotion faces, sequence cards from cereal boxes). For each TLM include: materials needed, preparation steps, target skills, how to use it, variations, skills it teaches, and cost estimate (aim for under Rs 50 each). Format as a resource manual with photos described. Consider Indian household and market availability, durability, safety, and sustainability.

prompts.title: Weekly Progress Graphing Template
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator teaching colleagues to visualize IEP progress data. I need you to design a complete progress graphing template and tutorial using free tools (Google Sheets or Excel) for tracking 4 IEP goals over 12 weeks for a student with moderate intellectual disability. Please provide: (1) Data entry spreadsheet layout (columns: date, goal, trial count, correct responses, percentage, prompting level, notes), (2) Formula specifications for auto-calculating percentages and running averages, (3) Graph creation instructions for line graphs with baseline phase, intervention phase, and aim line, (4) Color-coding system for different prompt levels, (5) Trend line interpretation guide (accelerating, decelerating, flat), (6) Decision rules embedded in sheet (when data shows 3 consecutive points below aim line, flag for review), (7) Dashboard summary view of all 4 goals, (8) Printable monthly report generator, (9) Step-by-step tutorial for teachers new to Google Sheets, (10) Troubleshooting common errors. Format as a how-to guide with spreadsheet specifications. Consider Indian teacher tech literacy levels, mobile phone compatibility for data entry, and offline usability.

prompts.title: Peer Buddy Program Implementation
prompts.prompt: Act as an inclusion coordinator launching a peer buddy program at a CBSE inclusive school in Chandigarh. I need you to design a complete peer buddy program pairing typically developing Grade 7 students with Grade 7 students who have disabilities (ASD, ID, LD). Please provide: (1) Program rationale and research base, (2) Buddy recruitment and selection criteria, (3) Parental consent forms for both groups, (4) 3-session peer buddy training curriculum (disability awareness using person-first language, communication tips, do's and don'ts, respecting dignity), (5) Structured interaction activities during lunch and free period, (6) Weekly schedule and supervision plan, (7) Buddy reflection journal template, (8) Recognition and incentive system, (9) Problem-solving protocol for difficulties, (10) Monthly program evaluation (buddy surveys, student well-being, teacher observations), (11) Parent communication, (12) Anti-stigma awareness components. Format as a program handbook. Consider Indian school hierarchies, avoiding savior narratives, ensuring mutual benefit, and alignment with RPwD inclusion principles and CBSE inclusive education guidelines.

prompts.title: Sensory Break Schedule for Classroom
prompts.prompt: Act as an occupational therapy consultant to an Indian primary school. I need you to design a whole-class sensory break schedule for a Grade 1 classroom of 38 students that includes 2 students with sensory processing challenges and several others who struggle with sustained attention. Please provide: (1) Rationale explained for teachers skeptical of sensory breaks, (2) Three daily scheduled breaks (mid-morning, after lunch, late afternoon) lasting 3-5 minutes each, (3) Specific activities for each break (movement breaks like cross-crawl, brain gym, yoga poses; calming breaks like deep breathing, stretching; alerting breaks like jumping, clapping games), (4) Indian cultural integration (simple yoga asanas, pranayama), (5) Teacher cue words and transition songs, (6) Visual timer usage, (7) Individual sensory breaks on-request protocol, (8) Teacher observation log to identify who needs more support, (9) Impact measurement (on-task behavior before and after), (10) Parent awareness letter explaining the benefits. Format as a classroom implementation guide. Consider hot weather limits on movement, small classroom space, and avoiding disruption to neighbors.

prompts.title: IEP Goals for Deaf/Hard of Hearing Student
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator with experience supporting deaf and hard-of-hearing students in Indian inclusive schools. I need you to draft comprehensive IEP goals for Aisha, an 8-year-old Grade 3 student with bilateral severe-to-profound hearing loss, uses hearing aids (ALIMCO supplied), knows basic Indian Sign Language, reads lips, enrolled in an English-medium school. Please provide: (1) Two communication goals (ISL development, spoken language support), (2) Two literacy goals (reading and writing at age-appropriate level), (3) One social goal promoting interaction with hearing peers, (4) One self-advocacy goal (requesting repetition, using assistive listening device), (5) Classroom accommodations list (preferred seating, visual aids, captioned videos, FM system, peer note-taker), (6) Related services (speech therapy, audiologist visits, ISL instruction), (7) Family training component for home signing, (8) Assistive technology recommendations available in India, (9) Teacher training brief on deaf education basics, (10) Progress monitoring tools. Format as an IEP document. Consider RPwD hearing disability provisions, availability of ISL resources through ISLRTC, and bilingual-bicultural approach.

prompts.title: Morning Routine Task Analysis and Visual Support
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator helping parents teach daily living skills to their child with autism at home. I need you to create a complete morning routine visual support system for Kabir, a 6-year-old with autism, currently requires full assistance for morning activities, family lives in a 2BHK in Mumbai. Please provide: (1) Task-analyzed morning routine from waking to leaving for school (15 steps), (2) Visual schedule using real photos of Kabir's actual bathroom and bedroom, (3) First-Then boards for difficult transitions, (4) Token board with reinforcement after routine completion, (5) Timer visuals for time-bound steps, (6) Teaching script for parents (backward chaining starting from the last step), (7) Prompt fading plan over 6 weeks, (8) Troubleshooting common issues (refusal, elopement, sensory avoidance of toothpaste), (9) Data recording chart for parents, (10) Photo taking and printing guide for budget-friendly creation, (11) Hindi labels option. Format as a parent-friendly toolkit. Consider Indian home layouts, joint family involvement, and bathroom practices (bucket bath, Indian toilet).

prompts.title: Inclusive Assessment Accommodations Matrix
prompts.prompt: Act as a special education coordinator developing an accommodations guide for an Indian inclusive school. I need you to create a comprehensive accommodations matrix for classroom and standardized assessments across different disability categories, aligned with CBSE CWSN guidelines. Please provide: (1) Matrix columns: disability category, presentation accommodations, response accommodations, setting accommodations, timing accommodations, aids allowed, (2) Rows for: SLD (dyslexia, dyscalculia, dysgraphia), ADHD, ASD, intellectual disability, visual impairment, hearing impairment, physical disability, (3) Specific accommodation examples for each cell (e.g., for dyslexia-presentation: oral reading of questions, enlarged print, colored overlays), (4) CBSE-approved accommodations vs school-level flexibility, (5) Documentation requirements (UDID card, psycho-ed assessment, doctor certificate), (6) Application procedure for board exams, (7) Common mistakes to avoid, (8) Student training on accommodation use, (9) Assessment integrity safeguards, (10) Teacher communication template. Format as a reference matrix with supporting explanations. Consider latest CBSE circulars, RPwD Act mandates, and practical implementation in 40-student classrooms.

prompts.title: Quarterly IEP Review Meeting Preparation
prompts.prompt: Act as a special educator preparing for a quarterly IEP progress review meeting. I need you to compile a complete meeting preparation package for Dhruv, a 10-year-old with ADHD and mild ASD, after the second quarter (October-December) of the academic year. His IEP has 7 goals across academic, behavioral, social, and self-regulation domains. Please provide: (1) Goal-by-goal progress summary with data graphs described (baseline to current, trend analysis), (2) Strengths observed this quarter with specific examples, (3) Challenges and hypotheses for lack of progress on specific goals, (4) Proposed IEP modifications (goal revisions, new strategies, added services), (5) Accommodations effectiveness review, (6) Teacher and staff input summary, (7) Parent input collection template sent home before meeting, (8) Student self-assessment form (age-appropriate), (9) Meeting agenda with time allocations, (10) Action items template, (11) Follow-up plan with responsible persons and dates, (12) Communication to non-attending team members. Format as a meeting preparation binder. Consider Indian academic calendar (April-March), exam period disruptions, and family holiday schedules around Diwali and Christmas.
